---
title:  
    "Geometric Modelling of Cumulative Variant counts"
date: 
    "Dec 26th, 2024"
author:  
    "Ali Movasati"  
output:  
    html_document:
        #css: style.css
        toc: true
        toc_depth: 2
        toc_float: true
        number_sections: true
        theme: lumen
        highlight: zenburn
        code_folding: hide
        code_download: true # to make the Rmd source code available
---


```{r loading libraries, echo=FALSE}

# loading libraries
library("ggplot2")
library("dplyr")
library("tidyr")
library("stringr")
library("magrittr")
library("rstatix")

```


```{r simulation}

# Simulating the process of drawing n_draws balls until all n_balls are observed
simulate_draws <- function(n_balls, n_draws, sampling_freq = 20) {
  unique_balls <- c()
  unique_balls_counts <- c()
  draws <- 0
  
  while (length(unique(unique_balls)) < n_balls) {
    draws <- draws + 1
    # Randomly sample 2 balls with replacement
    sampled_balls <- sample(1:n_balls, size = n_draws, replace = FALSE)
    unique_balls <- unique(append(unique_balls, sampled_balls))
    if (draws %% sampling_freq == 0){unique_balls_counts <- append(unique_balls_counts, length(unique_balls))} # to replicate sampling every 20th generation
  }
  return(unique_balls_counts)
}


# Simulate the process multiple times
set.seed(123) # For reproducibility
n_simulations <- 2
n_draws <- 4
n_balls <- 1000
results <- replicate(n_simulations, simulate_draws(n_balls, n_draws, sampling_freq = 1))
n_balls2 <- 1300
results2 <- replicate(n_simulations, simulate_draws(n_balls2, n_draws, sampling_freq = 1))
results <- append(results, results2)

```

```{r visualize simulations}

# Prepare the data for ggplot
plot_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(results), function(i) {
  data.frame(
    Draws = seq_along(results[[i]])*20,
    UniqueBalls = results[[i]],
    Simulation = factor(i) # Assign each list a unique identifier
  )
}))

# Plot using ggplot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Draws, y = UniqueBalls, color = Simulation)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) + # Line plot for each simulation
  geom_point(size = 2) +  # Add points for emphasis
  labs(
    title = "Unique Balls Over Draws in Simulations",
    x = "Draw Number",
    y = "Number of Unique Balls Observed",
    color = "Simulation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") # Use a distinct color palette



```


```{r fit model for simulation: method 1}


# Function to compute geometric CDF with parameter p
geometric_cdf <- function(x, p, scale) {
    scale*(1 - (1-p)^floor(x))
}

# x <- c(seq(from =0, length.out = length(results[[1]]), by = 20)) to account for 20 samling
x <- 1:length(results[[1]])
fit <- nls(results[[1]] ~ geom_cdf(x, p, scale), start = list(p = 0.1, scale = max(results[[1]])))

p_est <- coef(fit)["p"]
scale_est <- coef(fit)["scale"]

fitted_cdf <- geometric_cdf(x, p_est, scale_est)


df <- data.frame(
  x = x,
  Observed = results[[1]],
  Fitted = fitted_cdf
)

ggplot(df, aes(x = x)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = Observed, color = "Observed")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Fitted, color = "Fitted")) +
  labs(
    title = "Fit of Geometric CDF to Cumulative Data",
    x = "x",
    y = "Cumulative Probability"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Observed" = "blue", "Fitted" = "red")) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r measures of goodness-of-fit}


# Calculate R-squared
ss_tot <- sum((results[[1]] - mean(results[[1]]))^2)
ss_res <- sum((results[[1]] - geom_cdf(1:length(results[[1]]), p_est, scale_est))^2)
r_squared <- 1 - (ss_res / ss_tot)

# RMSE
n <- length(results[[1]])
rmse <- sqrt(sum((results[[1]] - fitted_cdf)^2) / n)
cat("RMSE:", rmse, "\n")

# AIC
aic_value <- AIC(fit)
cat("AIC:", aic_value, "\n")


```

```{r diagnostic plots}

# Calculate residuals
residuals <- results[[1]] - fitted_cdf

# Data for residual plot
residuals_df <- data.frame(
  Fitted = fitted_cdf,
  Residuals = residuals
)

# Residuals vs. Fitted Values Plot
ggplot(residuals_df, aes(x = Fitted, y = Residuals)) +
  geom_point(color = "purple", size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Residuals vs. Fitted Values",
    x = "Fitted Values",
    y = "Residuals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# Residuals vs. Predictors Plot
ggplot(data.frame(x = x, Residuals = residuals), aes(x = x, y = Residuals)) +
  geom_point(color = "darkgreen", size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Residuals vs. Predictor",
    x = "Generations",
    y = "Residuals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# QQ Plot of Residuals
ggplot(data.frame(SampleQuantiles = residuals), aes(sample = SampleQuantiles)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "QQ Plot of Residuals",
    x = "Theoretical Quantiles",
    y = "Sample Quantiles"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


```


####################


```{r fit model for data: method 1}
## counts object from mutation_accumulation.Rmd


data <- counts[counts$exp_line == "MT-4_1",]
View(data)
x <- 1:length(data$count)
fit <- nls(data$count ~ geometric_cdf(x, p, scale), start = list(p = 0.1, scale = max(data$count)))


p_est <- coef(fit)["p"]
scale_est <- coef(fit)["scale"]


# Generate the fitted geometric CDF values
fitted_cdf <- geometric_cdf(x, p_est, scale_est)

# Plot the observed cumulative data and fitted CDF


df <- data.frame(
  x = x,
  Observed = data$count,
  Fitted = fitted_cdf
)

ggplot(df, aes(x = x)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = Observed, color = "Observed")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Fitted, color = "Fitted")) +
  labs(
    title = "Fit of Geometric CDF to Cumulative Data",
    x = "x",
    y = "Cumulative Probability"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Observed" = "blue", "Fitted" = "red")) +
  theme_minimal()

# Calculate R-squared
ss_tot <- sum((df$Observed - mean(df$Observed))^2)
ss_res <- sum((df$Observed - geometric_cdf(1:length(df$Observed), p_est, scale_est))^2)
r_squared <- 1 - (ss_res / ss_tot)

```

```{r predictions beyond the scope of observed}
# Extend the range of x
x_extended <- 1:(length(x) + 100) # Add 10 more steps beyond the observed data

# Predict values for the extended range using the fitted parameters
predicted_cdf <- geometric_cdf(x_extended, p_est, scale_est)

# Create a data frame for plotting


df <- data.frame(
  x = c(x_extended),
  CDF = c(data$count, rep(NA, length(x_extended) - length(x))),
  Predicted = c(fitted_cdf, predicted_cdf[(length(x) + 1):length(x_extended)])
)

# Plot observed data, fitted values, and predictions
ggplot(df, aes(x = x)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = CDF, color = "Observed")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Predicted, color = "Predicted")) +
  labs(
    title = "Geometric CDF Fit and Predictions",
    x = "x",
    y = "Cumulative Data"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Observed" = "blue", "Predicted" = "red")) +
  theme_minimal()

```




####################




```{r fit model for simulation: method 2}
# Function to compute geometric CDF with parameter p
geom_cdf2 <- function(x, p) {
    1 - (1-p)^floor(x)
}


# Function to compute difference between your CDF values and geometric CDF
fit_error <- function(p, cdf_values) {
    x <- length(cdf_values)  # positions in sequence
    theo_cdf <- geom_cdf2(1:x, p)
    sum((cdf_values/max(cdf_values) - theo_cdf)^2)
}



# Find best p using optimization
best_fit <- optimize(fit_error, interval=c(0, 1), cdf_values=results[[1]])
best_p <- best_fit$minimum

# plot the fit
plot(results[[1]]/n_balls, x = c(1:length(results[[1]])))
a <- rgeom(n_balls, prob = best_p)
lines(ecdf(a), col = "blue")

# Calculate R-squared
ss_tot <- sum((results[[1]]/n_balls - mean(results[[1]]/n_balls))^2)
ss_res <- sum((results[[1]]/n_balls - geom_cdf2(1:length(results[[1]]), best_p))^2)
r_squared <- 1 - (ss_res / ss_tot)

```
